title: XIX
html:
XIX

In mentioning the word gay (as in the close of the last chapter) it puts one (i.e. an author) in mind of the word spleenп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝespecially if he has anything to say upon it: not that by any analysisп»ҝвҖ”or that from any table of interest or genealogy, there appears much more ground of alliance betwixt them, than betwixt light and darkness, or any two of the most unfriendly opposites in natureп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝonly вҖҷtis an undercraft of authors to keep up a good understanding amongst words, as politicians do amongst menп»ҝвҖ”not knowing how near they may be under a necessity of placing them to each otherп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝwhich point being now gainвҖҷd, and that I may place mine exactly to my mind, I write it down hereп»ҝвҖ”

Spleen

This, upon leaving Chantilly, I declared to be the best principle in the world to travel speedily upon; but I gave it only as matter of opinion. I still continue in the same sentimentsп»ҝвҖ”only I had not then experience enough of its working to add this, that though you do get on at a tearing rate, yet you get on but uneasily to yourself at the same time; for which reason I here quit it entirely, and forever, and вҖҷtis heartily at anyoneвҖҷs serviceп»ҝвҖ”it has spoiled me the digestion of a good supper, and brought on a bilious diarrhЕ“a, which has brought me back again to my first principle on which I set outп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝand with which I shall now scamper it away to the banks of the Garonneп»ҝвҖ”

вҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝNo;п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝI cannot stop a moment to give you the character of the peopleп»ҝвҖ”their geniusп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝtheir mannersп»ҝвҖ”their customsп»ҝвҖ”their lawsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝtheir religionп»ҝвҖ”their governmentп»ҝвҖ”their manufacturesп»ҝвҖ”their commerceп»ҝвҖ”their finances, with all the resources and hidden springs which sustain them: qualified as I may be, by spending three days and two nights amongst them, and during all that time making these things the entire subject of my enquiries and reflectionsп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

Stillп»ҝвҖ”still I must awayп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝthe roads are pavedп»ҝвҖ”the posts are shortп»ҝвҖ”the days are longп»ҝвҖ”вҖҷtis no more than noonп»ҝвҖ”I shall be at Fontainbleau before the kingп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ

вҖ”Was he going there? not that I knowп»ҝвҖ”п»ҝвҖ”п»ҝ
